title: 10 best herbal essential oils for bitter taste in mouth
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for bitter taste in mouth are natural remedies extracted from plants, which can alleviate an unpleasant and persistent bitter taste sensation in the mouth. These oils help by stimulating the mucous membranes in the mouth, throat, and tongue, reducing inflammation, and soothing discomfort. Examples of herbal essential oils that can help include peppermint, eucalyptus, and lemongrass, which can be used topically or inhaled. By using these oils, individuals with bitter taste disorders can improve their overall well-being, confidence, and quality of life, as they regain the ability to enjoy food and drinks without a lingering bitter aftertaste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Myrrh essential oils helps with bitter taste in mouth because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. When the tongue or mouth is infected by bacteria, it can cause an unpleasant bitter taste. The antimicrobial properties of myrrh oil help to eliminate these bacteria, reducing the bitterness and promoting a healthy mouth environment. Additionally, myrrh oil's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the mouth, further alleviating any discomfort or irritation associated with a bitter taste.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Myrrh essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause bad breath and bitter taste by reducing their growth and eliminating them from the oral cavity.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Myrrh essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, tongue, and other oral tissues, which can be caused by infection, injury, or gum disease, and contribute to a bitter taste.
Antioxidant properties: Myrrh essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the mouth and oral cavity from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to a bitter taste and dry mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which provide a strong, bitter flavor and aroma ideal for oral health.
Buds: Containing high concentrations of resinous compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and freshen the breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh commiphora myrrha resin from the tree and dry it properly for optimal oil extraction.
Grind 100 grams of dried commiphora myrrha resin into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered commiphora myrrha resin with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days at room temperature, shaking the jar daily to facilitate oil extraction from the resin.
Strain and decant the resulting oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with bitter taste in mouth because of its natural antibacterial properties that help to eliminate any lingering bacteria or toxins that may be causing the bitterness. Additionally, thyme oil's decongestant and anti-inflammatory qualities can reduce inflammation in the tongue and mouth, further alleviating bitter tastes. The oil's soothing properties also help to calm any irritation or discomfort caused by bitter substances, promoting a fresher and more pleasant oral environment.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme essential oil helps to combat bacteria that can cause bad breath and bitter taste by inhibiting their growth, reducing the severity of oral infections.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme essential oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the mouth, which can lead to a bitter taste sensation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with oral infections, gum disease, or other conditions that may cause bitter taste, promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the thyme plant are rich in sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its bitterness and earthy flavor, making them suitable for creating essential oils that alleviate bitter taste.

Buds: Thyme buds contain a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for their astringent and bitter properties, making them ideal for creating medicinal essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers carefully avoiding contamination and excessive moisture.
Steam distill the harvested plant material using a clevenger apparatus for approximately 4-6 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a clean glass container with a tight-fitting lid and label accurately.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove any impurities or debris.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and keep it away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with bitter taste in mouth because its potent menthol properties stimulate saliva production, washing away lingering bitter flavors. Additionally, peppermint's natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties soothe the tongue and mouth tissues, reducing inflammation that can contribute to bitterness. By calming and refreshing the mouth, peppermint oil eliminates bitterness, leaving a clean and invigorating sensation, perfect for revitalizing the senses after meals or when experiencing an unpleasant taste.
remedy_properties: Digestive aid: Peppermint oil helps to soothe digestive issues such as indigestion and bloating, which can contribute to a bitter taste in the mouth by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in peppermint oil, such as menthol and methanol, help to reduce swelling and irritation in the mouth and throat, which can alleviate a bitter taste caused by inflammation or infection.

Antimicrobial properties: Peppermint oil's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause bad breath, gum disease, and other oral health issues, ultimately reducing the likelihood of a bitter taste in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other compounds that help reduce bitterness in the mouth, promoting fresh breath and oral health.
Barks: Contain a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the tongue and mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried leaves of mentha x piperita at peak potency and aroma.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven or with air circulation for 24-48 hours to reduce moisture.
Crush the dried leaves into small pieces using a mortar and pestle or food processor to increase surface area.
Steam distill the crushed leaves in a still apparatus with pure water at a ratio of 1:5 for 2-3 hours.
Cool and separate the essential oil from the distilled water through decantation and filtering.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram essential oils helps with bitter taste in mouth because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the tongue and palate, reducing the perception of bitterness. Its calming effects also promote a healthy gut environment, which is linked to a balanced sense of taste. Additionally, wild marjoram's antimicrobial properties can help eliminate bad breath causing bacteria, leaving your mouth feeling fresh and clean.
remedy_properties: Digestive stimulant: Wild marjoram essential oil helps to stimulate digestion, which can reduce bitterness in the mouth by breaking down food properly and preventing the buildup of toxins.
Anti-inflammatory: Wild marjoram essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe inflammation in the mouth and throat that may be contributing to a bitter taste.
Antimicrobial: Wild marjoram essential oil has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat oral infections and bacteria that can cause bad breath and bitter tastes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wild marjoram contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to possess antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective against oral infections and reducing bad breath.
Buds: The buds of wild marjoram are rich in flavonoids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum vulgare leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Wash the harvested leaves gently with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the washed leaves using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius for 12 hours.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a stainless steel distillation apparatus for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a paper coffee filter into dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with bitter taste in mouth because its potent antimicrobial properties effectively reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause a bitter or unpleasant aftertaste. Additionally, sage's natural antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to the bitterness. As a result, sage essential oil can leave your mouth feeling fresh, clean, and free of any lingering bitterness, promoting a healthy and pleasant oral environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Sage essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to a bitter taste by reducing the production of saliva and alleviating irritation.
Antimicrobial property: Sage essential oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacteria and viruses that can cause bad breath and bitterness in the mouth, promoting a healthy oral environment.

Digestive stimulant property: Sage essential oil's digestive stimulant properties can help improve digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion and nausea, which can contribute to a bitter taste in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create bitter and astringent essential oils that can help reduce inflammation and improve digestion.
Barks: Containing high amounts of volatile oils, barks are prized for their bitter flavor and aroma, which can aid in soothing digestive issues and freshening the breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and stems from your garden or store-bought supply and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, dark place for 24-48 hours to remove excess moisture.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and transfer it to a glass jar with a lid containing 10 grams of chopped plant matter per 100 milliliters of carrier oil.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container.
Discard the solids and store the resulting essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, ready for use as a natural remedy for bitter taste relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cumin essential oils helps with bitter taste in mouth because it possesses antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that effectively combat the underlying causes of a bitter taste. It can help eliminate bad breath by reducing the growth of bacteria in the mouth, while its anti-inflammatory properties can soothe irritated taste buds. Additionally, cumin's natural carminative properties can also aid in digestion, which can further alleviate a bitter or metallic taste in the mouth.
remedy_properties: Digestive aid: Cumin's carminative and anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe digestive issues like indigestion and bloating, which can contribute to a bitter taste in the mouth. This is because cumin can reduce inflammation and ease discomfort in the gut.
Antimicrobial properties: Cumin's antimicrobial properties may help combat oral infections that can cause bad breath or bitter tastes, such as gum disease or tonsillitis.
Soothing and calming effects: Cumin's warm, comforting nature can help soothe mouth sores, ulcers, and other oral irritations that may contribute to a bitter taste.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the cumin plant contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, making them an effective ingredient in essential oils for bitter taste in mouth.
Barks: The barks of the cumin plant are rich in tannins and other compounds that can help to astringe and dry up mucous membranes, reducing bitterness and alleviating symptoms of oral infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cuminum cyminum seeds free from any contaminants or pesticides.
Dry the harvested seeds in a warm place for at least 7 days to reduce moisture content.
Blend one part dried cuminum cyminum seeds with two parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steam distill the blend using a still or a steam distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or coffee filter paper to remove impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood essential oils helps with bitter taste in mouth because of its potent antimicrobial properties. When used orally, wormwood oil can eliminate bacteria that cause bad breath and reduce inflammation in the tongue and gums, which can contribute to a bitter or unpleasant taste. Additionally, wormwood oil's natural antiseptic properties help soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing irritation and promoting a healthy oral environment that allows for a smooth, refreshing taste experience.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Herbal wormwood essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help kill bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause bad breath, gum disease, and other oral infections that contribute to a bitter taste in the mouth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the gums, tongue, and other oral tissues, which can alleviate a bitter taste caused by swollen or inflamed tissues.

Digestive stimulant properties: Herbal wormwood essential oil has been traditionally used to stimulate digestion, relieve indigestion and flatulence, and ease bloating, which can contribute to a bitter taste in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds, making them ideal for creating a strong, bitter flavor and aroma.
Barks: Contain a high concentration of thujone, which gives wormwood its distinctive bitter taste and is often used to stimulate digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried artemisia absinthium leaves and stems measuring one part of the herb to two parts of carrier oil.
Combine the harvested herb with jojoba oil in a dark glass jar with a tight-fitting lid at a ratio of 1:2.
Allow the mixture to steep for four hours at room temperature before straining it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter.
Transfer the filtered liquid into a clean amber glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place for seven days.
Decant the oil after seven days and discard any sediment or impurities before using the final product as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with bitter taste in mouth because of its unique properties. The oil contains flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects on the tongue and mouth tissues. This can help to reduce the sensation of bitterness by soothing and calming the mucous membranes. Additionally, pot marigold's antibacterial properties can help to eliminate bad breath-causing bacteria, leaving the mouth feeling fresh and clean. By using this essential oil, individuals can enjoy a smoother and more pleasant oral experience.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Calendula oil has antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial and fungal infections in the mouth, which can contribute to a bitter taste.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Calendula oil can reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, tongue, and throat, alleviating discomfort associated with bitter taste.

Antioxidant properties: As an antioxidant, Calendula oil helps protect the mucous membranes in the mouth from oxidative stress, which can cause inflammation and irritation that may lead to a bitter taste.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have been shown to be effective in reducing inflammation and alleviating bitter taste in the mouth.
Seeds: The seeds of the pot marigold plant are rich in fatty acids and vitamins, making them a valuable source of natural oils that can help to soothe and calm the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers at dawn when dew is still present and gently wash them with distilled water.
Dry the flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours or until they reach a moisture level of 10%.
Transfer 50g of dried calendula officinalis flowers to a glass jar and cover them with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or grapeseed oil.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to promote infusion, before straining the liquid through a cheesecloth into another container.
Transfer the extracted oil to a glass bottle and store it in an airtight container at room temperature, using within 6 months or until the desired shelf life is reached.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower essential oils helps with bitter taste in mouth because its natural compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, have potent antibacterial properties that combat bad breath-causing bacteria. These compounds also soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to a bitter or unpleasant taste. Additionally, Kansas coneflower essential oils' anti-inflammatory effects help reduce swollen tonsils and gums, allowing for a fresh and clean sensation in the mouth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Echinacea contains a variety of anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cinnamic acid and alkamides, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth and throat.
Antimicrobial properties: Echinacea has antimicrobial properties that may help to prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause bad breath or a bitter taste in the mouth.

Soothing mucilages: The roots and flowers of Echinacea contain mucilages, which are soft, gel-like substances that can help to soothe and protect mucous membranes in the mouth and throat, reducing discomfort and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower contain sesquiterpenes and other compounds that contribute to its bitter flavor and aroma, making them ideal for extracting essential oils for medicinal purposes.

Buds: Young buds on the Kansas coneflower plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide a distinctive bitter taste and can be used to create essential oils with anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia roots from a trusted supplier or your own garden.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 10 grams of the ground root powder with 50 milliliters of high-proof ethanol in a glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 24 hours, shaking occasionally, before filtering it through cheesecloth.
Distill the filtered liquid using a low-pressure steam distillation apparatus for 3 hours to produce a total of 10 milliliters of echinacea angustifolia essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wall marjoram essential oils helps with bitter taste in mouth because it has natural antibacterial properties that eliminate bacteria causing bad breath. The oil's decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the mouth, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with bitter taste. Furthermore, marjoram's sweet and herbaceous scent can help mask any lingering unpleasant odors, leaving your mouth feeling fresh and clean.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Marjoram essential oil has antibacterial properties that help eliminate bacteria that cause bad breath and a bitter taste, such as Streptococcus mutans.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Marjoram essential oil reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bitterness and discomfort.

Soothing and Calming Properties: Marjoram's soothing properties help calm irritation and discomfort in the mouth and throat, reducing the perception of bitterness and promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which provide a strong bitter flavor and aroma suitable for medicinal uses.
Seeds: Wall marjoram seeds have been traditionally used to make bitter-tasting remedies, and their essential oil is rich in compounds that can help soothe digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest teucrium marum leaves fresh in early morning after dew has evaporated and before sun reaches its peak intensity.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water and dry them gently with a paper towel to remove excess moisture.
Steam distill 500g of dried teucrium marum leaves in a 2l round-bottomed flask using a clevenger apparatus for 4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a glass vial and separate it from any impurities by filtering it through a sterile cotton wool plug.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat bitter taste in mouth with herbal essential oils is to mix a few drops of peppermint oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and swish it around your mouth for 30 seconds. Then, spit it out and rinse with warm water. The menthol in peppermint oil helps to neutralize the bitterness and leave your mouth feeling fresh and clean. Repeat this process as needed throughout the day.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bitter taste in mouth that are treated with herbal essential oils are bad breath, gum inflammation, and digestive issues. Peppermint oil helps freshen breath and soothe gums, while ginger oil alleviates digestive discomfort and inflammation. Thyme oil also treats bad breath by reducing bacterial growth and promoting a healthy oral environment. These oils can be used topically or inhaled to provide natural relief from these common ailments.
